summary: Today's AI landscape is marked by significant advancements in research, new model releases, and company announcements. Top universities and labs, such as Carnegie Mellon and Google DeepMind, are making strides in AI-driven astronomy and automated research. Meanwhile, companies like Netflix, DoorDash, and Meta AI are sharing insights into their AI engineering efforts and future plans. Hugging Face has unveiled new models and datasets, while OpenAI and Anthropic are focusing on practical AI applications and weak-to-strong supervision research.

highlights.title: Artificial Intelligence Research
highlights.insight: A new paper has been submitted and accepted to IJES 2026, covering topics in artificial intelligence, computers and society, and human-computer interaction. This research contributes to the growing body of knowledge in AI and its applications.

highlights.title: State of Routing in Model Serving
highlights.insight: Netflix has published a blog post discussing the state of routing in model serving, highlighting the importance of efficient model serving in AI applications. This insight provides valuable information for companies looking to optimize their AI infrastructure.

highlights.title: New Models and Datasets
highlights.insight: Hugging Face has unveiled new models and datasets, expanding its offerings in the AI community. These updates have significant implications for macOS agents and demonstrate Hugging Face's commitment to advancing AI research.

highlights.title: AI-Driven Astronomy Research
highlights.insight: Carnegie Mellon University has launched a new effort to advance AI-driven astronomy research, demonstrating the growing intersection of AI and space exploration. This initiative has the potential to revolutionize our understanding of the universe.

highlights.title: Automated Weak-to-Strong Researcher
highlights.insight: Anthropic has released a sandbox environment for weak-to-strong supervision research, along with datasets and baselines. This research aims to improve the efficiency and effectiveness of AI systems, with potential applications in various industries.

highlights.title: AI Advertising Plans
highlights.insight: Meta AI has announced plans to expand its AI tools across its advertising business, aiming to create more efficient and effective ad creation processes. This development has significant implications for the advertising industry and AI applications in marketing.

highlights.title: Tversky Neural Networks
highlights.insight: The Stanford AI Lab has announced a presentation on Tversky Neural Networks at ICLR 2026, showcasing advancements in AI research and its applications in neural networks. This research contributes to the growing body of knowledge in AI and its potential applications.

highlights.title: AI Budget Allocation
highlights.insight: Uber has allocated its entire AI budget for 2026 to Claude Code, demonstrating the company's commitment to AI research and development. This investment has significant implications for the future of AI applications in the transportation industry.

highlights.title: Agentic AI Hackathon
highlights.insight: MIT CSAIL has announced the Agentic AI Hackathon, an event focused on building AI agents that act autonomously. This hackathon provides a unique opportunity for researchers and developers to collaborate and advance AI research in autonomous systems.

highlights.title: AI and ML Archives
highlights.insight: DoorDash has published a collection of blog posts on AI and ML, highlighting the company's efforts in AI research and development. These posts provide valuable insights into the applications of AI in the food delivery industry and the importance of efficient AI infrastructure.

highlights.title: Practical AI Applications
highlights.insight: OpenAI has announced a focus on practical AI applications in 2026, aiming to develop more efficient and effective AI systems for enterprise use. This development has significant implications for the future of AI applications in various industries.
